story.iWant: threads to automatically generate meaningful titles
story.soThat: I can easily identify conversations in the thread list
story.tasks: - Detect 2nd exchange completion trigger (4 messages on main branch) - Generate title using LLM with first 2 exchanges as context - Update thread title in UI and backend (PATCH API call) - Implement manual title editing with inline input - Add title generation settings toggle - Handle edge cases (title exists, generation disabled, API errors)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Title generated after 2nd assistant response (4 messages total on main branch) (TM §4.1)
Title max 50 characters, truncated with ellipsis if longer (TM §4.2)
Title editable by user after generation (TM §4.3)
No duplicate generation calls for same thread (TM §4.1)
Skip if title already exists (manually set or previously generated)
Uses thread's configured model for generation
Generation happens asynchronously without blocking UI

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Generate thread titles after 2nd exchange. Inputs: Thread messages (first 2 exchanges). Outputs: Title string (50 char max). AI call, async, non-blocking.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: TitleGenerationRequest: {threadId, messages (4 total)}. TitleGenerationResponse: {title (max 50 chars), confidence, fallback}. Fallback: 'New Thread' if AI fails.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: After assistant response saved, check message_count = 2 user messages (2nd exchange complete), call AutoTitleService.generateTitle(), update thread title, emit event to refresh UI.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Auto-title generation: <3 seconds (AI call, async, non-blocking). Title generation success rate metric tracked.

artifact.reason: NEW - Title generation logic with AI integration
Existing component - needs title display and edit functionality
Existing service - needs trigger logic after 2nd exchange

story-context.constraints: - **Desktop Implementation:** Desktop-only. Title generated client-side, then synced to backend via PATCH API. - **Trigger Condition:** Exactly 4 messages on main branch (branchIndex = 0): 2 user + 2 assistant messages. - **Generation Logic:** Uses thread's configured LLM model. Prompt includes first 2 exchanges for context. Max 50 chars, truncate with "..." if longer. - **Async Execution:** Non-blocking. Generate in background, update UI when complete via store update. - **Idempotency:** Flag titleGenerationTriggered = true prevents duplicate calls. Skip if thread.title already set. - **Error Handling:** Fallback to "Untitled Thread" if AI fails or times out (>3s). Don't break thread on failure.

interface.name: TitleGenerationService.generateTitle()
interface.signature: generateTitle(threadId: string, messages: Message[]): Promise<string> - Calls LLM with first 2 exchanges, returns title (max 50 chars). Fallback 'Untitled Thread' on error.

interface.name: EditableTitle.svelte
interface.signature: Props: {title: string, onSave: (newTitle: string) => Promise<void>}. Renders title, click to edit, save on Enter, cancel on Escape.

interface.name: ThreadService.onMessageAdded()
interface.signature: Hook called after message added. Checks if 4 messages on main branch, triggers title generation if needed.

tests.standards: Use vitest for unit tests. Test trigger condition: 4 messages on main branch. Test title generation with mocked AI responses. Test truncation at 50 chars. Test fallback on AI failure. Test idempotency (no duplicate calls). Test manual editing flow.

test.idea: Unit test: Add 4 messages (2 user, 2 assistant) on main branch, verify generateTitle() called once
Unit test: Add 4 messages with branches (branchIndex != 0), verify title NOT generated (only count main branch)
Unit test: Mock AI returns 70-char title, verify truncated to 50 chars with '...'
Unit test: Thread has existing title, add 4 messages, verify no generation triggered
Unit test: Thread uses model 'gpt-4', verify AI call uses gpt-4 model
Integration test: Generate title, verify UI not blocked (can continue chatting while generating)
E2E test: Click title, edit to 'My Custom Title', press Enter, verify saved and displayed
Integration test: AI times out (>3s), verify fallback to 'Untitled Thread', thread remains functional
